title: If the Interceptor Missiles Run Out, It's Game Over
text: Hey there. So, today is Tuesday, March 3rd, 2026. This is the fourth day since the war began. So, last night I got some pretty bad news, which is that apparently the Gulf states here are running low on their interceptor missiles. So, those are what are saving our butts now because Iran is launching missiles towards basically everybody at this point. And so, uh these, uh, Gulf states Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, where I am United Arab Emirates have, uh, sophisticated anti-missile defense systems that shoot down the missiles that are coming in, and they are very effective. So, uh, on the first day, apparently, there were 65 Iranian missiles fired at Qatar, and 12 drone strikes. And of those one missile was not intercepted. One drone strike was not intercepted. So, they got 64 out of 65, and 11 out of 12. And the ones that, uh, weren't shot down didn't cause any serious damage. And so, we have been kept quite safe here, and that is, of course, extremely reassuring looking forwards if this is going to drag on for a while. But, if they run out of those interceptor missiles that changes the whole ballgame, and then we are wide open to genuine you know, attacks on the uh buildings out here, whether it's hotels or malls or just the US military base. And so then that would be the big question mark is will Iran in that case continue to primarily or hopefully only target the US military base, which is about 20 or 30 miles away from here. Or will they intentionally target you know, hotels, commercial districts, malls, etc. There's no way of knowing. You don't want to be in the position of finding out either way. And so yesterday was uh quite quiet. I don't recall any booms, any of the explosions, seeing any missiles overhead or anything until I was just about to go to bed. This was around, I think, 1:00 in the morning. And I heard a boom, looked out, saw the uh lights of missiles in the sky. They were shot down. More explosions. The uh hotel rattled here. And so that was very, very disconcerting. I thought, "Oh man, we might be in for a rough night." But uh as it turned out, it was quiet then or at least I didn't hear any if there was any more um of the uh missiles coming in. And then I slept through it, but I think that I would have most likely woken up to that. But regardless, that is a very, very precarious situation and really changes the equation as far as trying to consider my options, which are of course very, very limited. But uh if uh they run out of the missiles and then even if they aren't necessarily, you know, actively intentionally trying to bomb hotels and stuff like that. First, there's of course the possibility of just stray missiles, but then also if they bomb the airport, the runways, and flights can't get out, then you're really, really stuck. And then another consideration is that if those missiles do in fact run out, then there could be a rush to the border. And then it could be really tough to get through, hours of waiting, potentially with missiles, you know, flying around. And so at this point it is time to very seriously look into the possibility of getting out of here by land over the border into Saudi Arabia. It is about an hour's drive from here to the border. Now, the kind of most obvious closest possible place to go would be to Dammam, Saudi Arabia, which is a 4-hour drive from here. Get to the border and then another 3 hours from there to Dammam, or else to Riyadh. One big problem with that, which is that the US Embassy in Riyadh was hit. I'm not sure how badly damage there was, but as I mentioned in my last video, then Saudi Arabia is now seriously considering, or maybe already has, joined the uh fight against Iran, and so that then makes them a potential target for Iran, and no longer a potential target because they just got hit in Riyadh. And so it doesn't make sense to me to go to all this trouble of getting out of here and driving hours across the desert towards a Saudi Arabian city, which itself could then be a target, and then I could be in the exact same situation as I am now in which they're getting hit and the airport is closed down and I'm stuck waiting there. So, it makes a lot more sense to me to be heading south and ultimately trying to get into Oman, which as far as I know is fully safe, no strikes there and airports open in Muscat. And so that would be the best case scenario. That is a very, very long drive away across wide open desert and there are basically two potential routes. One is going through UAE and then one is going across Saudi Arabia across really stark desert. Looks like there's absolutely nothing whatsoever there. And then it is a solid, I don't know, 10 to 12 hours driving to get to Muscat, something like that. But it is time to seriously consider attempting that. And so I have been looking into it. I'm not going to say what the options are for now, but uh it is time to make a move as soon as possible. So, I think that is basically it for now. As I said, 2:00 in the afternoon now uh One little thing I'll mention that really doesn't even matter at this point, but maybe for others who are in the same situation then when I went down to pay the bill for the room to stay another night, then they said the government is in fact going to cover the cost. Just provide proof of a canceled flight. So, I've been making these uh reservations over the past several days for flights in the hopes that the airport opens up and then I have something reserved and ready to go rather than waiting until the airport opens and then almost certainly there is a rush on the reservations and then it could be harder to get a seat. Going to be more expensive, etc. And so as it turns out, the cancellations ended up being a bit advantageous as far as being proof of trying to get out of here, which I was able to show to the hotel reception desk and then get a free night stay. Now, that is not at all a priority for me at this time, but uh it is nice at least that they are making that offer and covering the uh cost of tourists who are trapped here. So, consider that. And so, on that subject then, it is a good idea, I think, to be making these reservations in case the airport does open up because apparently the airports in Dubai and Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, did open up, I think not fully but limited flights flying out of there. And so, that was giving some hope that maybe the Doha airport would also open up, but the latest uh update from Qatar Airways is just still closed, next update coming tomorrow. And so, that leaves me just hanging with no real hope at this point and that reality looming of they are saying they have about 4 days worth of the interceptor missiles left at this point. And then as it gets closer, if that becomes more, you know, certain that that is going to be the case, then you're going to have more people heading for the border. So, that is the situation for now. I am uh on it as far as trying to figure something out. Getting a way out of here and you'll find out in the next video. So, that's all for now. See you.
